meta.title: Meteor Showers - major + notable annual showers

iau_mdc.name: IAU Meteor Data Center (MDC) Shower Database
iau_mdc.role: Authoritative catalog: official IAU 3-letter codes + numbers, radiant RA/Dec, reference solar longitude, geocentric velocity Vg, parent bodies.
iau_mdc.citation: Jopek T.J. & Kanuchova Z. (2017), IAU Meteor Data Center - the shower database: A status report, Planetary and Space Science 143, 3-6.
iau_mdc.status: Public scientific catalog; measured facts, freely usable with citation.

imo.name: IMO Working List of Visual Meteor Showers (Table 5, 2026 IMO Meteor Shower Calendar)
imo.role: Source of the transcribed per-shower values: activity window, peak date, peak solar longitude, radiant alpha/delta (J2000.0), V_inf, r, ZHR.
imo.license: IMO Calendar/website content is Copyright (c) IMO (imo.net/legal-notice/). We do NOT redistribute the Calendar, its prose, charts or software. We ship only the underlying measured scientific facts (radiant, velocity, solar longitude, dates -- also in the IAU MDC and widely published) plus IMO's ZHR/r estimates, re-expressed in our own schema, crediting IMO. Per IMO guidance we mention the source.

ams.name: American Meteor Society meteor-shower calendar
ams.role: Cross-check only for peak dates / ZHR.
ams.license: Content (c) American Meteor Society; not copied.

units.peak_date: MM-DD, for the year 2026 (peaks drift ~+/-1 day between years; solar longitude is the stable quantity)
units.peak_solar_longitude: degrees (solar longitude at maximum; stable year to year)
units.zhr: IDEAL peak Zenithal Hourly Rate -- perfectly clear sky (limiting magnitude +6.5) WITH RADIANT AT ZENITH. Real observed rates are lower. null = IMO marks it 'Var' (variable / outburst-driven).
units.velocity_kms: V_inf, pre-atmospheric (apparent) entry speed in km/s from the IMO list. Close to but not identical with the IAU MDC geocentric velocity Vg (V_inf ~= sqrt(Vg^2 + 11.2^2)).
units.r_population_index: population index (dimensionless); lower = brighter mean magnitude. Typically 2.0-3.5.

meta.honesty: Radiant, activity dates, peak dates, peak solar longitude, velocity and parent bodies are REAL catalog data (IAU MDC + IMO Working List). ZHR is an IDEALIZED rate (radiant at zenith, perfect sky) -- actual observed rates are lower. Showers occur when Earth crosses a comet/asteroid debris stream; the radiant is a perspective effect. null = not well established; never invented.
meta.parent_cross_link: parent_in_catalog=true means parent_body's designation exists in public/data/small-bodies/objects.json (1P/Halley->ETA,ORI; 2P/Encke->STA,NTA; 21P/Giacobini-Zinner->DRA; 55P/Tempel-Tuttle->LEO; 109P/Swift-Tuttle->PER; 3200 Phaethon->GEM). 8P/Tuttle (URS) is NOT in that catalog, so URS does not cross-link.
meta.attribution: Meteor shower data: IAU Meteor Data Center (Jopek & Kanuchova 2017) and the IMO Working List of Visual Meteor Showers (2026 IMO Meteor Shower Calendar, ed. J. Rendtel); cross-checked with the American Meteor Society calendar.

showers.name: Quadrantids
showers.note: Very sharp peak (hours). Parent is asteroid (196256) 2003 EH1, an extinct-comet candidate (possibly linked to comet C/1490 Y1). ZHR is variable; IMO 2026 lists 80, historically quoted up to ~110-120.

showers.name: gamma-Ursae Minorids
showers.note: Minor far-northern shower; parameters tentative. Parent not established.

showers.name: alpha-Centaurids
showers.note: Southern-hemisphere shower; occasional bright fireballs. Parent not firmly established.

showers.name: April Lyrids
showers.note: Parent is long-period comet C/1861 G1 (Thatcher), ~415 yr orbit. ZHR usually ~18 but has briefly spiked (ZHR 90 in 1982).

showers.name: pi-Puppids
showers.note: Periodic/variable; rates tied to 26P/Grigg-Skjellerup returns. Southern shower.

showers.name: eta-Aquariids
showers.note: One of two showers from comet 1P/Halley (the other is the Orionids). Fast meteors; best from the tropics/southern hemisphere. Broad peak.

showers.name: eta-Lyrids
showers.note: Parent is comet C/1983 H1 (IRAS-Araki-Alcock), ~960 yr orbit.

showers.name: Daytime Arietids
showers.note: Strongest DAYTIME shower (radio/forward-scatter; radiant near the Sun). Parent debated (96P/Machholz complex / asteroid 1566 Icarus proposed).

showers.name: June Bootids
showers.note: Variable; near-silent most years but strong outbursts (1998, 2004) from comet 7P/Pons-Winnecke. Very slow meteors.

showers.name: July Pegasids
showers.note: Minor summer shower; parent not firmly established.

showers.name: July gamma-Draconids
showers.note: Sharp variable maximum (2016 outburst); slow meteors. Parent uncertain.

showers.name: Southern delta-Aquariids
showers.note: Parent is comet 96P/Machholz. Steady late-July stream; best from the southern hemisphere and low northern latitudes.

showers.name: alpha-Capricornids
showers.note: Parent is comet 169P/NEAT (= 2002 EX12). Slow, bright, yellow fireballs; low count but memorable meteors.

showers.name: eta-Eridanids
showers.note: Minor fast shower; parent not firmly established.

showers.name: Perseids
showers.note: The classic summer shower; parent comet 109P/Swift-Tuttle (~133 yr). Rich, fast, many trains. Best from mid-northern latitudes.

showers.name: kappa-Cygnids
showers.note: Slow shower with occasional fireballs; ~7 yr enhancement cycle. Asteroid 2008 ED69 proposed as parent (tentative).

showers.name: Aurigids
showers.note: Parent is long-period comet C/1911 N1 (Kiess), ~2000 yr orbit; produced outbursts in 1935, 1986, 1994, 2007. Fast meteors.

showers.name: September epsilon-Perseids
showers.note: Rich in bright meteors; 2013 outburst. Parent long-period, not identified.

showers.name: September Lyncids
showers.note: Minor shower; parent not established.

showers.name: Daytime Sextantids
showers.note: Daytime (radio) shower; likely part of the Phaethon/Geminid complex (asteroid 2005 UD proposed) -- association tentative, shipped as null.

showers.name: October Camelopardalids
showers.note: Brief north-circumpolar shower with a very sharp peak; long-period parent, not identified.

showers.name: Draconids
showers.note: Parent comet 21P/Giacobini-Zinner. Normally weak (ZHR ~5) but famous for storms (1933, 1946) and outbursts (2011, 2018). Very slow meteors.

showers.name: epsilon-Geminids
showers.note: Weak, fast; overlaps the Orionids so care is needed to separate them. Parent not established.

showers.name: Orionids
showers.note: The second shower from comet 1P/Halley (with the eta-Aquariids). Fast meteors, broad plateau; some years reach ZHR 40-70.

showers.name: Leonis Minorids
showers.note: Minor fast shower; long-period comet parent (C/1739 K1 proposed), tentative -- shipped as null.

showers.name: Southern Taurids
showers.note: Part of the Taurid complex from comet 2P/Encke. Slow, bright fireballs over a long, low-rate activity window (the 'Halloween fireballs').

showers.name: Northern Taurids
showers.note: Northern branch of the Taurid complex (comet 2P/Encke; asteroid 2004 TG10 also associated). Slow fireballs; enhanced 'swarm' years.

showers.name: Leonids
showers.note: Parent comet 55P/Tempel-Tuttle (~33 yr). Very fast; famous for ~33-yr storms (e.g. 1833, 1966, 1999-2002). Modest ZHR ~15 in non-storm years.

showers.name: alpha-Monocerotids
showers.note: Variable; rare brief outbursts (ZHR ~400 in 1925, 1935, 1985, 1995) from a long-period parent, not firmly identified.

showers.name: November Orionids
showers.note: Minor shower near the Orionid radiant region; parent not established.

showers.name: Phoenicids
showers.note: Variable/periodic; parent comet 289P/Blanpain (= 2003 WY25). Slow meteors; 1956 outburst. Southern shower.

showers.name: Puppid-Velids
showers.note: A complex of poorly-separated southern radiants; the listed position is a reference, not a single true maximum. Parent(s) not established.

showers.name: December Monocerotids
showers.note: Parent proposed as comet C/1917 F1 (Mellish) (tentative). Weak; care needed to separate from Geminids and sigma-Hydrids.

showers.name: sigma-Hydrids
showers.note: Fast; reliably reaches ZHR 5-8 with some bright meteors. Parent (long-period comet) not firmly established.

showers.name: Geminids
showers.note: Parent is ASTEROID (3200) Phaethon, a 'rock comet' -- unusual: most streams come from comets. The best and most reliable annual shower; medium speed, many bright meteors and fireballs.

showers.name: Comae Berenicids
showers.note: Long, low-rate stream (merged with the December Leonis Minorids in the IMO list). Fast meteors. Parent not firmly established.

showers.name: Ursids
showers.note: Parent comet 8P/Tuttle. North-circumpolar; often overlooked (peaks just after the Geminids). NOTE: 8P is not (yet) in the small-bodies catalog, so this parent does not cross-link.
